steps.title: Understand the x-intercept
steps.content: The x-intercept of a function is the point where the graph crosses the x-axis. This occurs where the $y$-value equals zero.

steps.title: Read the x-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Examining the grid, locate where the line intersects the x-axis. The line crosses at $x = -12$, so the x-intercept is the point $(-12, 0)$. That is the correct answer.
